## mfa_activity

Daily multi-factor authentication (MFA) activity, broken down by factor type and
outcome.

Each row represents the count of MFA attempts for a single combination of day,
MFA factor type, and result.

`Keywords`: MFA, multi-factor, second factor, OTP, FIDO2, authentication, security

### Provenance

Queried directly from the IBM Verify production instance via API.

* **Updated**: Daily, at 6am America/Toronto
* **Steward**: EDCP Data and Research Team
* **Contact**: [Yinlin Zhang](mailto:yinlin.zhang@cds-snc.ca)

### Fields

One row per day per `mfa_type` per `result`.

| Field Name | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| from_date | timestamp | Start of the reporting day (midnight) |
| to_date | timestamp | End of the reporting day (23:59:59) |
| mfa_type | string | The MFA factor used. One of "sms_otp", "fido2", "kq", "totp", "voice_otp", "qr", "ibm_verify_push", "email_otp" |
| result | string | Outcome of the MFA event. One of "success", "failure", or "sent" |
| count | integer | Number of MFA attempts for this day, factor, and result |
| fetched_at | timestamp | When the aggregation last fetched this row |
| date | string | The reporting day as a text date (for example, "2026-05-31"). Mirrors `from_date` |

### Notes

Only OTP factor types (`sms_otp`, `email_otp`, `voice_otp`) generate a "sent"
event, because they are issued before the user responds. Successfully clearing
one of these challenges therefore counts as two events: first a "sent" event,
then either a "success" or "failure" event depending on how the user responds.
It is possible to generate multiple "success"/"failure" events on a single
"sent" event if the user inputs their OTP incorrectly first and then correctly.
Non-OTP factor types (`fido2`, `kq`, `totp`, `ibm_verify_push`) have no "sent"
event and produce only "success"/"failure" rows.

When calculating MFA usage metrics, de-duplicate accordingly: count "sent"
events for OTP factors (sending an email OTP but not using it is still usage),
and "success"/"failure" events for non-OTP factors. Summing "sent" alone would
silently drop every non-OTP factor.

When calculating success rate metrics, use the number of successful events
divided by the sum of the success and failure events, excluding "sent".
